title: Hash Tables, Collision by Collision
tagline: Chaining, open addressing, robin hood — see where keys actually land
summary: Hash tables look magical from outside: average O(1) lookup, no ordering, used in every dictionary, set, and cache. Inside, the magic is collision resolution. Insert keys into a small hash array and watch them collide; toggle between chaining and three open-addressing strategies (linear, quadratic, robin hood); watch the load factor creep up and the lookups slow down.
highlights: Insert keys live into a small hash table; watch them collide and resolve under four strategies.
Toggle between separate chaining, linear probing, quadratic probing, and robin-hood hashing.
Load-factor meter and average-probe-length counter make the O(1)-amortised story concrete.
Watch a degenerate hash function turn O(1) into O(n) on adversarial keys — and why hash randomisation defeats it.
Cuckoo, hopscotch, and consistent hashing pointers for when you need stronger worst-case or distributed guarantees.
